text.title: Certificate Rotation Pipeline (cert-manager / Terraform / Compose) Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in production. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Pre-commit security scanner flags `BEGIN PRIVATE KEY` in repo; key has been rotated 4 times but historical commits still hold the original.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated the cert + key as 'just config' and committed both.
antipattern.fix: Rotate the affected cert immediately; revoke old cert at issuer; rewrite Git history with BFG / git-filter-repo; add pre-commit hook scanning for `BEGIN PRIVATE KEY|BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY`.

antipattern.symptom: Operator runs `kubectl apply -f cert.yaml` directly; renewal config is silently wrong; cert expires 60 days later.
antipattern.root-cause: Bypass of the IaC pipeline; no render-scan-apply.
antipattern.fix: Lock RBAC so only the CI service account can apply Certificate CRDs; route every change through render-scan-apply CI.

antipattern.symptom: Cert expires at 03:00 UTC Saturday; first user-visible 502 wakes on-call at 09:00.
antipattern.root-cause: Prometheus rule never wired; cert-manager metrics scraped but not alerted on.
antipattern.fix: Add alert rule on `certmanager_certificate_expiration_timestamp_seconds - time() < 7*86400`; route to PagerDuty.
